meta.title: Theo Bayenet · Front-End Engineer
meta.description: Front-End Engineer with 4+ years of experience building complex SaaS products. Strong front-end architecture, product mindset, available for a full-time position remote or on-site.

home.subtitle: Front-End Engineer
home.description:
Where products become complex,
I design interfaces that remain simple to use.

about.title: Who am I?
about.text1:
🔧 Self-taught, I started coding because building applications fascinated me and nothing was stopping me.
Very quickly, I had the opportunity to grow in a demanding technical environment that accelerated my progress far beyond what I could have achieved alone.
about.text2:
🎯 Four and a half years later, I approach front-end with a product mindset: understanding the domain, anticipating usage, and building interfaces that truly make sense.

These experiences also taught me how to collaborate, understand business challenges, and work with people from very different backgrounds.
about.text3: 🗄️ Today I’m strengthening my fullstack profile, because understanding the whole picture makes you a better contributor at every level

development.title: Front-End Architecture
development.description: Scalable components, design systems, polymorphic interfaces

precision.title: Business complexity
precision.description: Conditional rules, multiple states, tenant-based logic

performance.title: Performance & Quality
performance.description: Optimization, strict TypeScript, code reviews, standards

skills.title: Technical Expertise

1.name: React / TypeScript
1.description: Strong expertise in React and TypeScript for building maintainable, type-safe applications. Daily use for over 4.5 years on complex SaaS products.

2.name: Next.js
2.description: Building React applications with Next.js: rendering strategies, App Router, and SEO optimization. Experience working in monorepos with Turborepo.

3.name: Angular · Vue
3.description: 2 years of experience working on Angular applications (RxJS, modules, guards) as well as Vue. Comfortable navigating between ecosystems without friction.

4.name: Tailwind · Shadcn/ui
4.description: From isolated SSR-compatible components to multi-axis design systems: light/dark modes, glassmorphism, color palettes, all combinable. <themesMenuTrigger>→ Try it live</themesMenuTrigger>

1.name: Quality & Robustness
1.description: A constant focus on maintainability: decoupled, readable, predictable, and well-documented code with a clear architecture. Each module has a well-defined and limited responsibility, making future changes easier.

2.name: Performance
2.description: Bundle optimization, lazy loading, and code splitting. Real-world case: reduced the main bundle by 88% on a critical legacy Angular application.

3.name: Typed & Context-Aware Interfaces
3.description: Designing interfaces whose structure reflects the discriminated unions of the business domain: forms, validation, and UI vary depending on the business case. Each domain variation produces a distinct and adapted user experience.

4.name: AI-Augmented Development
4.description: I leverage AI to accelerate development and focus on high-value tasks, while staying vigilant about the quality of generated code.

1.name: Cross-Stack Contribution
1.description: Contributing to backend modeling decisions to ensure the data structure accurately reflects business cases and allows the interface to fully leverage it.

2.name: A/B Testing & Analytics
2.description: Using GTM/GA4 and Hotjar for behavioral tracking and user journey analysis. UX decisions driven by data rather than intuition.

3.name: Mentoring & Team Spirit
3.description: Supporting teammates and sharing best practices to help grow the team’s skills.

4.name: Cross-Functional Collaboration
4.description: Experience working in distributed teams, agile rituals, and feedback-driven processes. Collaborating with diverse roles (product owners, designers, backend engineers, support) to build shared vision and effective solutions.

skills.additional-info: Constantly evolving, I continue to strengthen my <primary>frontend</primary> expertise while actively expanding into <secondary>backend</secondary> development.
